steps.title: Relate q to t
steps.content: The original model uses $t$ in years. Since there are 4 quarters per year, $q$ quarters equals $t = \frac{q}{4}$ years.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $M = 2200 \times (1.03)^{q/4}$ — matches the substitution since $t = q/4$. Correct.
  • B) $M = 2200 \times (1.03)^{4q}$ — would mean $t = 4q$, treating each quarter as 4 years. Wrong.
  • C) $M = 2200 \times (1.0075)^{4q}$ — uses a different base 1.0075 with exponent $4q$, giving $(1.0075)^{4} \approx 1.0303$ per quarter cycle of 4, not the correct per-quarter growth. Wrong.
  • D) $M = 2200 \times (1.126)^{q}$ — treats each quarter as a full year of growth at rate 12.6%, which contradicts the 3% annual rate. Wrong.
That confirms A is correct.
